mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in > 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 500k signals robust labor market recovery and consumer income expansion. This drives consumption demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in > 50% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900k units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth > 5% year-over-year signals synchronized demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors. This joint expansion predicts positive equity returns as forward earnings guidance improves. The mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 926k and RSAFS at 436,772M as of 2020-09-15
failure_condition: When both HOUST > 900k and RSAFS YoY growth > 5%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side contraction or demand weakness. In a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2%), this contraction predicts negative equity returns as it indicates economic slack and reduced corporate pricing power over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 and CPIAUCSL at 235.74 as of 2020-10-15
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM and CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers a negative equity return signal over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline due to lower consumer spending and employment uncertainty.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating quarter-over-quarter above 3% annualized signals strong economic expansion. This expansion drives corporate earnings revisions upward and equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in > 40% of rolling 8-quarter periods.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY growth) rising above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate future rate hikes and earnings margin compression from input cost inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.74 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2020-10-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 2% month-over-month signals supply-side expansion and capacity utilization improvement. This supply expansion, when paired with rising retail sales (RSAFS), indicates firms can meet demand without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 2% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 and RSAFS at 436,772M as of 2020-10-15
failure_condition: When INDPRO MoM growth > 2% and RSAFS MoM growth > 1%, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.
